source.title: Context-dependent computation by recurrent dynamics in prefrontal cortex.

confidence_rationale: High-quality empirical paper combining macaque PFC single-unit recordings with a trained recurrent-network model that reproduces the population dynamics. The paper demonstrates that PFC implements context-dependent input selection at the population-dynamics level: the same physical sensory input is integrated toward choice when context flags it as task-relevant and is suppressed when context flags it as irrelevant. This is direct evidence that the rule/context signal is read out as input-selective gating rather than as a uniform multiplicative gain across all input or candidate dimensions, which is the closest empirical analogue available for the A1 landing in SD-033a. Held below 0.85 because the task is two-input integration, not multi-candidate trajectory selection -- the input-selection result generalises to candidate-selection only by analogy, not by direct mapping.
failure_signatures: The Mante 2013 result concerns selection among input dimensions (motion vs colour), not among multiple action candidates. If the bias projection in SD-033a operates on output candidates (trajectories) rather than input dimensions, the analogy from input-selective gating to per-candidate bias is one ARC of indirection -- and a uniform-gain readout is in principle still consistent with input-selective integration upstream.

The trained-RNN account argues that selection and integration are unified within a single dynamical process. If the V3 SD-033a abstraction (a static rule_state held in lateral PFC then projected via a single Linear layer) is too static to capture the dynamical-process-level selection mechanism Mante 2013 proposes, neither the A1 landing nor the A1 alternative may be the right level of description; SD-033a may be missing a recurrent gating component.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Macaque prefrontal cortex implements context-dependent computation by selecting and integrating noisy sensory inputs through population-level recurrent dynamics; the same neurons participate in both selection and integration, with context determining which sensory dimension is integrated toward the choice. A trained recurrent network reproduces the population dynamics, indicating that selection and integration are two aspects of a single dynamical process unfolding within the same prefrontal circuits.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-033a's A1 question asks whether the lateral-PFC-analog projects a per-candidate bias vector or a uniform scalar gain into E3 trajectory selection. Mante 2013 provides the closest empirical analogue: in macaque PFC, the rule/context signal is read out as input-selective gating, not as a uniform multiplicative gain. Different input dimensions are differentially weighted as a function of the current context. Translated to SD-033a's selection problem, this favours the per-candidate landing -- the rule_state should differentially weight candidates rather than scale them uniformly. The mapping is by analogy from input dimensions to output candidates; the strict empirical claim is about input selection, not candidate selection.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Mante 2013 measures selection among two input dimensions (motion direction vs colour) on each trial. SD-033a's A1 question is about per-candidate bias on multiple trajectory alternatives. Translating from 'input-selective gating' to 'per-candidate bias' requires the assumption that PFC's selection machinery generalises across the input/output asymmetry, which is plausible but not directly demonstrated. The paper is also silent on whether the same dynamics operate in lateral vs medial PFC subdivisions, which matters for SD-033a's 'lateral-PFC-analog' specificity.
mapping.source_context: Two macaques, dorsolateral PFC single-unit recordings (~727 neurons) during a context-dependent perceptual decision task with two interleaved contexts (motion-relevant and colour-relevant). Population analyses + trained RNN modelling. Core claim is at the population-dynamics level rather than single-cell.

confidence_components.notes: Very high source quality (Nature 2013, large primate dataset, RNN modelling validates the dynamical interpretation, foundational for context-dependent computation literature). Mapping fidelity good but not perfect because the input-selection -> candidate-selection generalisation is by analogy. Transfer risk moderate because the dynamical-process-level mechanism may not map cleanly onto SD-033a's static rule_state -> Linear bias projection abstraction; the V3 SD-033a may be capturing only the surface signature of a deeper recurrent mechanism.
